ID:       ddx-06b77652
Title:    ADR-style: 7 reliability principles for ddx try / ddx work / sub-agent execution
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, area:agent, area:server, kind:design, reliability, bead-quality
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-e34994e2
Desc:     The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.

The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.

PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS

P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

P3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

P4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.

P5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.

P6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).

P7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)
- Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children)

INTERSECTIONS
- Each refactor child (ddx-c8f79963 C5, ddx-06eb05d8 C7, ddx-9228a484 C9, ddx-848069a3 C8, ddx-b669bb9f C6, ddx-c670ef0a C12) gets an AC line citing the principle(s) it enforces
- Bead-quality audit applies P7 to ~20 existing open beads

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and supports P7 / bead-quality.
Accept:   1. docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md exists and contains all 7 principles with their statements above (verbatim).
2. README sections of each refactor child (C4, C5, C6, C7, C8, C9, C11, C12, C13) cite the principle(s) the child enforces. Cross-link in those bead descriptions added via ddx bead update.
3. CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md updated with one-line pointers: 'See docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.'
4. cd cli && go test ./... still green (no code change; sanity check that nothing broke).
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
6. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].

data.output:
# Bead Authoring Template

> A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent
> sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation.
> — Reliability principle **P7** (`docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`)

This document is the canonical template + rubric for creating beads in
this repository. It exists because the 2026-05-03 bead-quality audit
(`.ddx/executions/20260503T155638-bead57f0cb9e/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md`)
found that only **1 of 20** representative open beads scored 8/8 on
the sufficient-sub-agent-prompt rubric. The most common failure modes
were: no specific test names in AC (80 % miss), no `go test` +
lefthook gate in AC (80 % miss), and no root cause with file:line in
description (65 % miss).

---

## 1. The 8-criterion rubric

Every bead must satisfy:

| # | Criterion | Verifier |
|---|-----------|----------|
| (a) | **Title**: one-line scope clarity, imperative, names subsystem + change | "Pagination off-by-one in bead list endpoint" not "Pagination bug" |
| (b) | **Description**: problem + ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE + proposed fix + non-scope | Root cause cites `cli/path/file.go:42-50` |
| (c) | **AC**: numbered, verifiable, includes specific test names | At least one AC names `TestFoo` or a `go test -run` filter |
| (d) | **AC** includes "wired-in" assertion when introducing new code paths | New function → AC asserts a caller invokes it (production or test) |
| (e) | **AC** includes `go test` command + lefthook gate | Both `cd cli && go test ./...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` named |
| (f) | **Labels**: phase + area + kind + cross-refs | `phase:N`, `area:*`, `kind:*`, plus adr/spec/prevention if applicable |
| (g) | **Parent + Deps**: explicit | Parent set; deps either listed or "no deps" stated |
| (h) | Description reads as **sufficient sub-agent prompt** | A competent agent given only the bead body can pick a file and run tests without asking |

Score 8/8 = execution-ready. Less = retrofit before dispatch.

---

## 2. Template fields

```text
TITLE
  <area>: <imperative one-line scope>
  e.g. "agent: unify ExecuteLoopSpec across cobra/HTTP/server layers"

TYPE
  task | bug | epic | chore

LABELS
  phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>
  + cross-refs: prevention, adr:<id>, spec:<id>, story:<n>, refactor

PARENT
  <ddx-id> of governing epic / story / spec
  (omit only if this is a root)

DEPS
  - <ddx-id-1>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  - <ddx-id-2>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  (or explicit "No deps.")

SPEC-ID (when applicable)
  --set spec-id=FEAT-NNN  | SD-NNN | TD-NNN | ADR-NNN

DESCRIPTION
  PROBLEM
    <one-paragraph statement of what is broken or missing>

  ROOT CAUSE
    <function/file:line where the defect lives>
    <what the code does today vs what it should do>
    For new features: ROOT CAUSE -> "no existing implementation"
    is OK; replace with CURRENT STATE describing the gap.

  PROPOSED FIX
    <bulleted concrete changes; cite file:line for each>
    - cli/internal/foo/bar.go:120-145  rename Baz -> Quux
    - cli/internal/foo/bar_test.go      add TestQuux_RoundTrip

  NON-SCOPE
    - <thing readers might assume is included but isn't>
    - <thing covered by sister bead ddx-NNNN>

  INTERSECTIONS (optional)
    - <ddx-id>: <how this bead relates to that one>

ACCEPTANCE
  1. <verifiable assertion 1 — names a Test, file path, command, or
     observable artifact>
  2. <verifiable assertion 2>
  3. ...
  N-1. cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green.
  N. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 3. Examples

### 3.1 Example of a strong bead (audit score 8/8)

`ddx-1e516bc9` — *"fizeau Execute: explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower must route within harness's eligible models"*

Why it scored 8/8:

- **Title (a)** names subsystem (`fizeau Execute`), the precondition (`explicit Harness + empty Model`), and the expected behavior (`route within harness's eligible models`).
- **Description (b)** opens with **OBSERVED** (one paragraph, file:line) → **SCOPE — UPSTREAM (fizeau) ONLY** → **INVESTIGATION FIRST** (with explicit close-as-no-op outcome) → **UPSTREAM CHANGE** (file:line into `service_execute.go:193-224`, `models.yaml:31-60`, `registry.go`) → explicit **NOT IN SCOPE** list naming five excluded paths.
- **AC (c)** names two concrete tests:
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_RoutesWithinHarness` and
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelNoProfile_FailsClearly`.
- **Wired-in (d)**: AC #6 asserts the resolved model surfaces in
  `.ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json`, `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json`, and the
  workers UI — three distinct call-graph endpoints.
- **Test command + lefthook (e)**: AC #9 — `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.`
- **Labels (f)**: `phase:2, area:agent, kind:fix, upstream-fizeau, observed-failure`.
- **Parent + Deps (g)**: parent `ddx-e34994e2`; dep `ddx-cfedee8e` with explanation that the ladder must supply MinPower for fizeau to act on.
- **Sufficient (h)**: a sub-agent given only the bead body knows which file in fizeau to read, what the gap looks like, what the negative-test outcome should be, and how to verify the end-to-end fix without asking.

### 3.2 Example of a weak bead (audit score 1/8) — annotated

`ddx-0793ac75` — *"federation: federated GraphQL resolvers + routing-metadata fields"*

Verbatim description:
```
GraphQL resolvers that aggregate across spokes (federatedWorkers,
federatedRuns, federatedDocuments, etc.). Add routing-metadata fields
exposing which spoke owns each result. Write-routing contract: hub
forwards mutations to owning node, never broadcasts (Story 15
dependency).
```

Verbatim AC:
```
1. federated* resolvers in schema.
2. Routing-metadata visible per result.
3. Mutations route to owner; tested.
4. Tests cover happy + degraded paths.
```

Failures, criterion by criterion:

- **(b) FAIL** — no problem statement (what's broken today?), no root cause, no file:line, no non-scope. The reader cannot tell where the schema lives, where resolvers go, or what "routing-metadata" should look like as a Go type.
- **(c) FAIL** — "tested" and "Tests cover happy + degraded paths" name no `Test*` function. Sub-agent has no way to know what to write or where.
- **(d) PARTIAL** — "tested" implies the resolvers are wired, but no production-call-graph assertion (e.g., "schema.graphql Mutation block exposes field X").
- **(e) FAIL** — no `go test` command, no lefthook gate.
- **(h) FAIL** — a sub-agent reading this cold cannot answer: which schema file? which resolver package? what fields on routing-metadata? what's the wire format? Hand-curation by the operator is the only path forward.

**Retrofit shape** (illustrative — not an actual edit):
```
PROBLEM
  Story 14 needs aggregate-across-spokes resolvers. Today the GraphQL
  schema (cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql) exposes only
  per-node Worker/Run/Document queries. Federation requires
  federated* counterparts that fan out to spokes and stitch results.

ROOT CAUSE
  No federated resolver layer exists. cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go
  contains only single-node resolvers. Spoke fan-out is in
  cli/internal/server/federation_spoke.go but nothing in the GraphQL
  layer calls it.

PROPOSED FIX
  - schema.graphql: add Federated{Worker,Run,Document} types with
    `ownerSpoke: String!` field.
  - graphql_resolver.go: add federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
    federatedDocuments resolvers calling federation_spoke.FanOut.
  - graphql_mutation.go: write-routing helper routes mutations to
    owning node via federation_spoke.RouteToOwner.

NON-SCOPE
  - New non-federated resolvers (out: covered by Story 13).
  - Spoke discovery (out: covered by ddx-fe63969f).

ACCEPTANCE
  1. schema.graphql exposes federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
     federatedDocuments queries with ownerSpoke metadata.
  2. TestFederatedWorkers_FanOutAggregates verifies fan-out + stitch.
  3. TestFederatedMutation_RoutesToOwner verifies write-routing.
  4. TestFederatedWorkers_DegradedSpoke returns partial results +
     warning.
  5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
  6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 4. Authoring checklist (paste into your editor before running `ddx bead create`)

```text
[ ] (a) Title: imperative, one line, names subsystem + change
[ ] (b) Description has all four sub-elements:
        [ ] PROBLEM (one paragraph)
        [ ] ROOT CAUSE with file:line citation (or CURRENT STATE for new features)
        [ ] PROPOSED FIX (bulleted, file:line per item)
        [ ] NON-SCOPE
[ ] (c) Each AC line is verifiable; at least one names Test* or `go test -run`
[ ] (d) New code path → AC asserts a production or test caller invokes it
[ ] (e) AC includes:
        [ ] cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green
        [ ] lefthook run pre-commit passes
[ ] (f) Labels: phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>, +cross-refs
[ ] (g) Parent set; deps listed or "no deps" stated
[ ] (h) Self-test: re-read the bead from cold. Could a competent
        sub-agent execute it without asking a question? If no, retrofit.
[ ] No /tmp/ paths cited as load-bearing context. Inline the relevant
    excerpt instead — /tmp does not survive between machines.
```

---

## 5. Anti-patterns surfaced by the audit

- **`/tmp/` references as load-bearing context.** 5 of 20 audited beads cite a `/tmp/...` plan file. These are functionally broken links to a sub-agent on a different machine. **Inline the excerpt.**
- **"See parent" without inline summary.** Refactor children of `ddx-5cb6e6cd` (C4/C7/C11/C13) systematically defer detail to the parent epic. Each child fails the stand-alone test. **Inline the parent's relevant 5-10 lines.**
- **Vague test phrases.** "Tests cover X." "All execute-bead tests still pass." Both omit the actual `Test*` name or `-run` filter the executor needs.
- **Story-doc copy-paste.** One-paragraph beads (`ddx-0793ac75`, `ddx-0e5c3005`, `ddx-1d52a30c`, `ddx-0ca4ffda`) are story excerpts that were never expanded for execution. Story documents are *upstream* of beads, not the bead itself.
- **Implicit lefthook.** Lefthook runs in pre-commit hooks anyway, but if AC doesn't name it, executors omit it from their own verification before committing.

---

## 6. When to skip a criterion

- **Epic beads** are aggregates by design. Criterion (c) (specific test names) and (d) (wired-in) commonly pass via children. The epic should still satisfy (a), (b), (e) — pointing at the children's collective `go test` set — and (f), (g), (h).
- **Doc-only beads** legitimately omit (c) (no tests) and (d) (no code path). They must still satisfy (e)'s lefthook clause and (h).
- **Pure deletion / rename beads** legitimately omit (d). They must still cite the deletion-target's file:line in (b) and assert byte-identical behavior preservation in (c).

---

## 7. References

- `docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md` (planned via `ddx-06b77652`) — P7 source.
- `.ddx/executions/20260503T155638-bead57f0cb9e/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md` — the audit that produced this template.
- `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/beads.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/reference/beads.md` — operational guidance on creating beads via `ddx bead create`.

CLAUDE.md:210:Bead descriptions + AC must be sufficient context for a sub-agent to execute the work without operator hand-curation (principle P7, `docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`). Before invoking `ddx bead create`, walk the 8-criterion rubric in `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md`: title clarity, root cause with file:line, numbered AC with specific test names, wired-in assertion, `cd cli && go test` + lefthook gate, labels with cross-refs, parent + deps, standalone description (no `/tmp/*`, no chat references). Beads scoring < 7/8 will fail in autonomous drain.
docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md:5:> — Reliability principle **P7** (`docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`)
docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md:229:- `docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md` (planned via `ddx-06b77652`) — P7 source.

Title:    C5: move no_changes adjudication, decomposition, push-failed, push-conflict into try.Attempt (absorb ddx-b24e9630 + ddx-c6e3db02 behavior)

Priority: 1

Parent:   ddx-5cb6e6cd
Deps:     ddx-358f2457, ddx-b24e9630, ddx-c6e3db02, ddx-09d2990c
Desc:     PROBLEM
execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036 handles no_changes adjudication, decomposition, push-failed, and push-conflict inline in the loop body. These are distinct outcome behaviors that belong in try.Attempt so the loop only sees Disposition + Cooldown + ParkReason + RecordEvents.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036: the block that follows a bead attempt contains outcome-specific branches (no_changes, decomposition, push_failed, push_conflict) as raw loop logic rather than Disposition values from try.Attempt.
- ddx-b24e9630 (no_changes verification): adjudicateNoChangesContract function exists in the codebase — confirm it is called from try.Attempt's no_changes branch (not merely defined). If wired: preserve. If unwired: this child must wire it.
- ddx-c6e3db02 (rate-limit retry): EvaluateRetryPolicy (or similar) must be called from try.Attempt's rate-limit detection path — confirm actual wiring before this refactor moves it.
- try/attempt.go:67 is the candidate location for the relocated no_changes adjudication logic.
- The dead-code audit (ddx-09d2990c) must close before this child to establish which functions are genuinely wired vs. only defined.

PROPOSED FIX
- Remove lines 875-1036 from execute_bead_loop.go; replace with: read Disposition + Cooldown + ParkReason + RecordEvents from try.Attempt result.
- Move no_changes adjudication (verification_command runner + triage label logic) into try.Attempt's no_changes branch near try/attempt.go:67.
- Move rate-limit retry (Retry-After honor + exponential backoff + budget cap) into try.Attempt's rate-limit detection.
- Status NEVER changes to needs_investigation on no_changes; stays in canonical state 6; uses triage:no-changes-unverified label per ADR-004.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not modify the Drain loop contract beyond replacing lines 875-1036 with Disposition/Cooldown reads.
- ddx-09d2990c wire-or-delete must close first (hard dep).
Accept:   1. execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036 removed; equivalent logic in try.Attempt with proper Disposition/ParkReason returns.
2. ddx-b24e9630 behavior preserved in try.Attempt: verification_command runner invoked from no_changes branch near try/attempt.go:67; triage:no-changes-* labels applied.
3. ddx-c6e3db02 behavior preserved in try.Attempt: Retry-After honor + exponential backoff + budget cap in rate-limit detection path.
4. Wire-confirmation: adjudicateNoChangesContract and EvaluateRetryPolicy confirmed called from try.Attempt (not just defined) — grep assertions in test file.
5. TestAttempt_NoChanges_StaysCanonical_NotNeedsInvestigation verifies status does not become needs_investigation.
6. TestAttempt_RateLimit_Retry_HonorsRetryAfter verifies Retry-After header honored.
7. C0 fixture diff: no_changes_verified, no_changes_unjustified, decomposition, push_failed, push_conflict lifecycles byte-identical.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

### ddx-06eb05d8
ID:       ddx-06eb05d8
Title:    C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps + pick() derives from drain.result
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   phase:2, refactor, kind:refactor
Parent:   ddx-5cb6e6cd
Deps:     ddx-b669bb9f
Desc:     PROBLEM
  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.

### ddx-9228a484
ID:       ddx-9228a484
Title:    C9: first-class StopCondition enum + cost-cap relocation (absorbs ddx-cfedee8e + ddx-a8718bec + ddx-dc157075 behavior)
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   phase:2, refactor, kind:refactor
Parent:   ddx-5cb6e6cd
Deps:     ddx-848069a3, ddx-cfedee8e, ddx-a8718bec, ddx-dc157075, ddx-09d2990c
Desc:     PROBLEM
Execute-loop cost-cap and stop logic is scattered across executor closure in cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (lines 1722-1730, 1818-1850) and ad-hoc loop continuation checks. Stopping conditions (Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, Blocked) are evaluated implicitly with no enum, making the stop logic brittle and hard to extend. The escalation ladder (escalation.Ladder.Next) exists in cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go but has no production caller — dead-code audit (ddx-09d2990c) must confirm before this child can safely wire or delete it.

ROOT CAUSE
- Cost cap: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1722-1730 checks max_cost inside executor closure; cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1818-1850 has a second cost-cap site. Neither site uses a StopCondition type.
- Escalation: cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go:30 defines Ladder.Next; no call site in production confirmed (dead-code audit required per ddx-09d2990c).
- Reviewer cost (from ddx-a8718bec): reviewer cost is tracked in a separate accumulator inside drain but not evaluated in a unified stop gate.
- Worker stay-alive tactical (from ddx-dc157075): poll-interval default is 0 (drain mode); the tactical default of 30s should come from C10 (ddx-f9acb86d) but cost-cap relocation must happen first.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go defining StopCondition enum: Drained | Once | Signal | Budget | NoProgress | Blocked.
- Add drain.stop(state DrainState) StopCondition — evaluates all stop conditions including cost cap.
- Remove cost cap from executor closure (agent_cmd.go:1722-1730, 1818-1850); move into drain.stop budget evaluation.
- Relocate ddx-cfedee8e MinPower-ladder behavior into try.Attempt Retry path (wire Ladder.Next or confirm deletion from dead-code audit).
- Relocate ddx-a8718bec reviewer cost accumulator into drain.stop budget evaluation.
- Subsume ddx-dc157075 tactical changes.
- After cost-cap consumers audit: find all dashboard/alerting consumers of execution_failed with max-cost reason; document in .ddx/executions/<run-id>/cost-cap-consumers.md.

NON-SCOPE
- Poll-interval default (30s) belongs to C10 (ddx-f9acb86d).
- Frontend changes.
- Removal of ddx-cfedee8e / ddx-a8718bec / ddx-dc157075 beads (operator closes those after confirming subsumption).
Accept:   1. cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go defines StopCondition enum: Drained | Once | Signal | Budget | NoProgress | Blocked.
2. drain.stop(state) returns StopCondition.
3. Cost cap removed from executor closure (agent_cmd.go:1722-1730 and 1818-1850 sites eliminated); lives only in drain.stop.
4. ddx-cfedee8e MinPower-ladder behavior relocated into try.Attempt Retry path; escalation.Ladder.Next confirmed wired (or deleted if dead-code audit finds it unwired).
5. ddx-a8718bec reviewer cost handling relocated into drain.stop budget evaluation.
6. ddx-dc157075 tactical changes subsumed (verify by checking dc157075 ACs against this child's implementation).
7. Downstream-consumer audit: grep -r 'execution_failed.*max-cost' cli/ (excluding tests); findings committed to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/cost-cap-consumers.md.
8. TestStopCondition_Budget_StopsBudget verifies cost-cap-tripped produces StopBudget.
9. TestDrain_CostCapTripped_StopsBudget verifies drain terminates on StopBudget.
10. C0 fixture diff: cost-cap-tripped lifecycle event stream updated to show StopBudget, not execution_failed.
11. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
12. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

### ddx-b669bb9f
ID:       ddx-b669bb9f
Title:    C6: single commitOutcome helper deletes 18 store-error exit paths + handleOutcomeStoreError
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   phase:2, refactor, kind:refactor
Parent:   ddx-5cb6e6cd
Deps:     ddx-c8f79963
Desc:     PROBLEM
The execute-bead loop body (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036) has 18 store-error exit paths — every bead outcome write can hard-exit the drain loop. This violates the loop's contract: a single bead's store failure should never kill the entire drain worker.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036 contains ~14 direct error returns triggered by store write failures, all routing through handleOutcomeStoreError. Each returns a hard error to Drain instead of scheduling a cooldown and continuing. The helper handleOutcomeStoreError itself is also a terminal-exit path. The 18 exit paths (14 store-error sites + handleOutcomeStoreError variants) are the dominant smell: 18 of 24 returns in the loop body are store-error paths.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/agent/work/commit.go defining commitOutcome(ctx, store, beadID, op func() error).
- commitOutcome calls op(); on error, calls SetExecutionCooldown with 'loop-error' ParkReason and returns nil (never hard-errors to Drain).
- Delete handleOutcomeStoreError.
- Replace all 14 callsites in execute_bead_loop.go (pre-refactor coordinates: 628, 740, 763, 800, 834, 850, 869, 877, 905, 918, 963, 979, 1014, 1026) with commitOutcome.

NON-SCOPE
- Logic changes to what's stored (only how store errors are handled).
- execute_bead_loop.go file rename (separate bead ddx-1d867ec1).
Accept:   1. cli/internal/agent/work/commit.go (or cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go equivalent) defines commitOutcome(ctx, store, beadID string, op func() error) error.
2. handleOutcomeStoreError deleted from execute_bead_loop.go.
3. All 14 callsites in execute_bead_loop.go (pre-refactor: 628, 740, 763, 800, 834, 850, 869, 877, 905, 918, 963, 979, 1014, 1026) replaced with commitOutcome.
4. TestCommitOutcome_StoreErrorContinuesLoop verifies that a store write failure schedules cooldown and returns nil (loop continues).
5. TestCommitOutcome_StoreError_SchedulesCooldown_NotExit verifies cooldown event is recorded with ParkReason=loop-error.
6. C0 fixture diff: store-glitch-during-close lifecycle byte-identical event stream (loop-error event present, no hard exit).
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

### ddx-c670ef0a
ID:       ddx-c670ef0a
Title:    C12: single phase-event emission (replaces 4 emit sites with state-machine-driven emitPhase)
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, refactor, kind:refactor
Parent:   ddx-5cb6e6cd
Deps:     ddx-9228a484
Desc:     PROBLEM
Phase events (queueing, running, terminal, bead-result JSONL) are emitted from 4 separate sites in execute_bead_loop.go. Adding a new phase or changing event shape requires coordinated edits across all 4 sites, introducing subtle divergence. Today the JSONL-on-disk shape is only verified by manual inspection against a baseline fixture.

ROOT CAUSE
The 4 emission sites in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:
- :577 — queueing phase event
- :596 — running phase event
- :1076-1091 — terminal phase event (with Disposition/ParkReason)
- :1093 — bead.result JSONL write

Each site constructs and emits events independently with no shared Phase enum or emitPhase abstraction. The C0 fixture diff approach (byte-identical JSONL baseline) is the only correctness gate, but it is not automated.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/agent/work/phases.go defining Phase enum: Queueing | Running | Terminal.
- Add emitPhase(ctx, store, beadID string, phase Phase, outcome Outcome) error called by state transitions.
- Replace the 4 current emission sites with calls to emitPhase.
- Verify JSONL on-disk shape is byte-identical to the C0 baseline (automated fixture diff in test).

NON-SCOPE
- Changing the JSONL event format or adding new event types.
- Renaming execute_bead_loop.go (separate bead ddx-1d867ec1).
Accept:   1. cli/internal/agent/work/phases.go defines Phase enum (Queueing | Running | Terminal) + emitPhase function.
2. Four emission sites in execute_bead_loop.go (:577, :596, :1076-1091, :1093) replaced with emitPhase calls.
3. JSONL on-disk shape byte-identical to C0 baseline (automated fixture diff asserts this in test).
4. TestEmitPhase_Terminal_WritesCorrectEvent verifies terminal event has Disposition and ParkReason fields.
5. TestEmitPhase_Running_BeforeTerminal verifies event ordering.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

### ddx-f339c399
ID:       ddx-f339c399
Title:    evidence: ddx-29058e2a no_changes outcome validates P7 (bead-quality > execution-machinery hypothesis)
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 3
Labels:   phase:2, area:beads, kind:evidence, reliability, bead-quality
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-06b77652
Desc:     EVIDENCE
2026-05-04 02:24:15: 'ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex' returned no_changes after one attempt. Bead description was NOT in the retrofit batch (audit recommended it but it was held back to test exactly this prediction). Same codex, same machinery, succeeded earlier on retrofit'd beads ddx-aee651ec and ddx-3e60fd84.

This is strong evidence for the P7 (bead-as-prompt) hypothesis from RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES (ddx-06b77652): bead authoring quality is a dominant factor in execution success, possibly more dominant than the execution machinery itself.

CONTRAST
- Non-retrofit ddx-29058e2a (multi-file scope, 'see also' references, no concrete starting point): no_changes
- Retrofit ddx-aee651ec (concrete file paths, named types): success at commit ?? (closed 2026-05-03 20:46)
- Retrofit ddx-3e60fd84 (file:line cites, named tests): success at commit ?? (closed 2026-05-03 22:24)

CONCLUSION
The 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) Path B-vs-C question (sub-agent + verbatim bead vs sub-agent + hand-curated) has a partial answer already: when bead is well-authored, sub-agent + verbatim bead succeeds; when bead is poorly authored, even capable executors return no_changes.

NEXT STEPS
1. Retrofit ddx-29058e2a per audit recommendations (separate operator work)
2. Re-dispatch ddx-29058e2a via codex after retrofit
3. If retrofit'd ddx-29058e2a succeeds: P7 hypothesis fully validated; comparison study Path B-vs-C answered
4. Audit + retrofit remaining open beads on the dispatch path (so future dispatches don't waste codex usage on no_changes outcomes)

NON-SCOPE
- Closing ddx-29058e2a (separate retrofit + re-dispatch)
- Modifying the 3-path comparison study (this is partial evidence; full study still planned post-refactor)
- Changing the bead-authoring template (no template change indicated yet)
Accept:   1. This bead exists with the evidence above committed to the bead store.
2. Linked from RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead (ddx-06b77652) as evidence for P7.
3. Linked from comparison-study bead (ddx-31f745cd) as partial Path B-vs-C data.
4. Closed once the post-retrofit re-dispatch of ddx-29058e2a confirms P7 hypothesis.

No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX

NON-SCOPE

Reviewed and approved by user before any implementation beads are filed from the roadmap.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:14:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4025192","closing_commit_sha":"fabf513096b522e921020e323ef1bf4a9cf36afa","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:43:27.404796076Z","description":"Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

PROPOSED ARCHITECTURAL SHIFT
WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API. --local mode collapses to "worker registers with itself" using an in-process implementation of the same API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

THE ADR EVALUATES AND CHOOSES AMONG

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

OUTSTANDING BEADS THIS ADR MUST RECONCILE (overlap analysis required in the ADR output)

ABSORBED (implementation subsumed by the new architecture; ADR explicitly closes these as superseded once roadmap lands):
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): becomes the API request/response payload — the spec IS the wire format. Refactor concern dissolves.
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): ALREADY FIXED at commit 41cb762e via flat 30s poll-interval default + attempted-map reset. Whether the new architecture preserves these tactical fixes or replaces them via API-driven semantics needs an ADR decision.
- ddx-cfedee8e (escalation: wire ladder): the ladder becomes server-side orchestration logic, not loop-body logic. ADR must specify where ladder lives in the new shape.

CONFLICTS WITH IN-FLIGHT REFACTOR (refactor epic ddx-5cb6e6cd children) — ADR must resolve sequencing:
- ddx-c8f79963 (C5: no_changes adjudication move into try.Attempt): refactors the SAME code that the API transition refactors. Either land C5 first then transition, OR fold C5 into the transition.
- ddx-06eb05d8 (C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps): same surface; same choice.
- ddx-848069a3 (C8: routing preflight to Drain startup): related to "server-side preflight" decision in API design.
- ddx-9228a484 (C9: StopCondition + cost-cap, absorbs ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-dc157075): heavy overlap. ADR must decide whether StopCondition becomes a server-side concept, a worker-side concept, or both.
- ddx-387a0178 (C13: file/identifier rename + ddx try CLI command): the ADR's new package layout (cli/internal/agent/work/) may conflict with C13's rename plan. Coordinate.
- ddx-1d867ec1 (rename execute_bead_loop.go): same as C13.

PARALLEL-SAFE (no conflict; ADR notes them but doesn't reshape):
- ddx-5b3e57f4 (cooldown: distinguish disrupted from gave-up): the API contract makes disruption naturally observable (server sees worker disconnect → marks Disrupted). But the cooldown logic itself is independent. ADR should call out that the API design enables cleaner Disrupted classification.
- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute upstream gap): independent of API design.
- ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1, ddx-055e8d32 LAYER 2, ddx-5ae050dc LAYER 3 (GraphQL project scoping): all in cli/internal/server/graphql/. Independent of worker API.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker bug): already fixed.
- ddx-a946c744 + ddx-aee651ec + ddx-3e60fd84 + ddx-83440482 (REACH/checks): hook into pre_merge gate; needs to integrate with the new worker contract but doesn't block ADR.
- All Story 6/14/15/16 frontend + backend feature beads: independent.

SPEC UPDATES THIS ADR DRIVES (the ADR roadmap must include these as deliverables)

- New ADR document: docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — major amendment: worker API contract section, --local-collapses-to-API explanation, deprecation of direct execution paths
- FEAT-010 (Executions) — amendment: worker lifecycle now spans across server restarts; "execution" becomes a bead-attempt regardless of which worker performs it
- SD-024 (Resolved Config / CLIOverrides) — possible amendment: if ResolvedConfig becomes part of the API request payload, SD-024 reshapes
- TD-030 (Backend interface + chaos contract) — cross-reference if any
- CONTRACT-003 (Fizeau Service) — no change required (fizeau is below the worker; the API is between worker and ddx-server, not worker and fizeau)

NOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS ADR
- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads from the roadmap)
- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)
- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)
- Reshaping the bead schema (out of scope; ADR-004 owns that)

OUTPUTS (TD deliverables)
- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- Updated FEAT-006 (or proposed amendments listed)
- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown
- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly
- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups
- Overlap reconciliation table: each overlapping bead (above) classified as ABSORBED / CONFLICT-RESOLVED / PARALLEL-SAFE with explicit rationale

SEQUENCING DIRECTIVE (per user direction, 2026-05-03)
- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing
- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads
- C5/C7/C9 explicitly FROZEN until the ADR's roadmap establishes the new package layout","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:38.009514865Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260503T021424-dac100b6","harness":"claude","input_tokens":21,"output_tokens":14799,"total_tokens":14820,"cost_usd":1.0767455,"duration_ms":249728,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:38.0358447Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=14820 cost_usd=1.0767"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"","resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:43.263235583Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"erik","body":"APPROVE

This bead closes as 'investigation complete; see child beads for fixes'. ddx-b808df39 references this bead's findings in its implementation notes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T04:31:48Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"1d0b3e373a618623062909694332536c74f2630a","created_at":"2026-04-18T03:01:36.338860593Z","description":"## Context

ddx-b808df39 (P0: ddx agent stop/kill command for hung workers) treats hung-worker remediation as an operational need, but does NOT investigate the UNDERLYING cause of the hangs. The feature is a useful escape hatch, but shipping it without understanding WHY workers hang means we'll keep needing the escape hatch.

Opus fresh-eyes review (2026-04-18) flagged: 'Hung workers for 33h–142h are an operational defect independent of root cause' — the feature bead is justified, but a SIBLING root-cause investigation is missing from the portfolio.

## Observed

The referenced 33h and 142h hang durations in ddx-b808df39 imply multiple incidents where an execute-bead worker process remained in-progress past any reasonable upper bound for a single bead. Needs to investigate:

1. Is the worker process alive but stuck in a tool call (bash subprocess that never returns)?
2. Is it alive but mid-LLM-call with no timeout (provider hang + no client-side deadline)?
3. Is it dead but the bead's claimed_at never cleared (tracker-side leak)?
4. Is the worktree locked or fsync'd in a way that prevents cleanup?

## Fix direction

This bead is an INVESTIGATION, not a fix. Deliverable is a short report that:

1. Reproduces or observes at least one hang incident and captures:
   - ps / pgrep state of the worker process tree
   - stack traces via 'go tool pprof' against the worker (if alive)
   - Timeline from .ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/*.jsonl (last event type + timestamp)
   - tracker claimed_at and retry_after state

2. Classifies the hang into one of the four categories above (or a new category).

3. Files concrete follow-up beads for each identified root cause:
   - Tool subprocess timeout if bash hangs
   - LLM client deadline if provider hangs
   - Claim expiry / heartbeat if tracker-side leak
   - File-lock / worktree cleanup if fs-side

4. Recommends whether ddx-b808df39's stop/kill mechanism is sufficient as mitigation or needs additional primitives.

## Relation to ddx-b808df39

ddx-b808df39 ships the OPERATOR TOOL to stop a hung worker. This bead finds the BUG that causes hangs. Both are needed; ship ddx-b808df39 first if the operator-tool is more urgent, then this investigation.

## Non-goals

- Fixing the root cause in this bead. Scope is investigation + follow-up bead filing.
- Preventing all possible hangs (impossible). No live hung worker was available\n+in this isolated worktree to capture live `ps`/`pprof`, so the incident evidence is\n+drawn from `.ddx/executions/*` bundles and the worker/runner source path.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 1. Observed evidence (from `.ddx/executions/`)\n+\n+| Metric                                               | Count / value                                        |\n+| ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |\n+| Total attempt bundles                                | 328                                                  |\n+| Bundles with `result.json`                           | 291 (89%)                                            |\n+| Bundles with `manifest.json` but **no** `result.json` | 37 (11%) — executions whose worker never finalized  |\n+\n+Top five longest *finalized* attempts:\n+\n+| `duration_ms` | Wall time    | `detail`                                                                    | Bundle                          |\n+| ------------- | ------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |\n+| 7,720,908     | **2h 8m**    | `agent: compaction stuck: 50 consecutive failed compaction attempts`        | 20260416T230316-efbf91c5        |\n+| 7,200,098     | **2h 0m**    | `timeout after 2h0m0s` (claude streaming wall-clock fired)                  | 20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba        |\n+| 6,764,927     | **1h 53m**   | success (agent harness, qwen)                                               | 20260416T230315-f9f46822        |\n+| 5,649,987     | **1h 34m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260416T230313-e193942b        |\n+| 4,284,347     | **1h 11m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260415T103422-bb3a45fa        |\n+\n+**Key observation:** the 33h / 142h hang durations cited in ddx-b808df39 are\n+~15× and ~65× longer than the longest *finalized* attempt on record. This means\n+the hung workers never reached the point of writing `result.json` — they were\n+blocked inside `runner.Run` (or deeper) for days. Every code path that\n+*should* have terminated them (idle timeout, compaction-stuck, stall detector,\n+cancel propagation) failed to fire.\n+\n+The 37 manifest-only bundles are candidates for hung or externally-killed\n+workers; e.g. `20260416T215953-6b515c05` has a manifest from 2026-04-16T21:59Z\n+with no result. Those fit the profile of \"workers running 33h–142h with no bead\n+assigned\" reported on agent projects.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 2. Root-cause classification (source code evidence)\n+\n+The investigation checked the four hypotheses from the bead description.\n+\n+### RC1 (PRIMARY) — Caller context is discarded inside the Runner\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (`Runner.Run`):\n+```go\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+```\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (`Runner.RunAgent`):\n+```go\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+```\n+\n+`RunOptions` (types.go) takes no `context.Context` at all. `agent.ExecuteBead`\n+likewise does not accept ctx, and `server.WorkerManager.runWorker` passes its\n+own ctx into `worker.Run(ctx, …)` but that ctx **never reaches the agent\n+invocation**.\n+\n+**Impact:** `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (workers.go:648) calls `handle.cancel()`\n+which cancels only the worker goroutine's ctx. The running agent provider call\n+/ subprocess is detached. The only way to interrupt a stuck agent today is a\n+SIGKILL from outside the process. This is the structural defect that enables\n+33–142h hangs — once the agent is stuck, nothing inside DDx can stop it.\n+\n+### RC2 — Idle-reset timer, not wall-clock deadline\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/executor.go:35-40` creates an *idle timeout* context value:\n+```go\n+func withExecutionTimeout(ctx context.Context, timeout time.Duration) context.Context {\n+    return context.WithValue(ctx, executionTimeoutKey{}, timeout)\n+}\n+```\n+\n+`executor.go:154-178` consumes it and **resets the timer on every stdout/stderr\n+byte**. `agent_runner.go:264-285` implements the same pattern against agentlib\n+events.\n+\n+The default timeout is 2h (`types.go:363 DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000`). The\n+comment claims \"2 hours — long enough for any task; supervisor/agent decides\n+termination\" but the actual behaviour is **\"2 hours of silence required to fire\"**.\n+A provider that emits a heartbeat, a ping, a retry line, or any byte resets the\n+timer indefinitely.\n+\n+The `runClaudeStreaming` path (`claude_stream.go:373-384`) is the only path that\n+uses a wall-clock deadline — and it is the only path we see actually hitting\n+the timeout (bundle 20260415T015231 exactly 2h 0m 0.098s).\n+\n+**Impact:** for `harness=agent` (qwen, minimax, openrouter, etc.), no wall-clock\n+bound exists. 33–142h hangs are consistent with a provider that dribbles events\n+slowly enough to defeat the idle reset.\n+\n+### RC3 — Compaction-stuck breaker fires late\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:218-241` increments a counter on each no-op\n+compaction event and trips at `stuckThreshold = maxConsecutiveCompactionFailures`\n+(50 by default). On observed `efbf91c5` it took **2h 8m** to trip. If one\n+iteration takes 3 minutes to produce a no-op compaction event, 50 events =\n+~150 min — matches the observed tail.\n+\n+**Impact:** on its own, the breaker is too slow to be a useful hang-guard. It\n+should be replaced or complemented by a wall-clock-bounded variant.\n+\n+### RC4 — Provider HTTP requests likely lack a per-request deadline\n+\n+`RunAgent` passes the internal `context.Background()`-derived ctx into\n+`agentlib.Run(ctx, req)`. The agent library's providers (`openai.Client`,\n+anthropic client) do not appear to install a per-request deadline themselves\n+(grepping the v0.3.14 module cache shows short timeouts on discovery/probe\n+only; the main call path has no WithTimeout). A stalled TCP socket after\n+headers are received but before the response body arrives can hang a streaming\n+read indefinitely.\n+\n+**Impact:** consistent with \"alive but mid-LLM-call with no client-side\n+deadline\" — hypothesis (2) in the bead description. Cannot be fully confirmed\n+from source alone; needs runtime `go tool pprof http://localhost:<port>/debug/pprof/goroutine`\n+against a live hung worker.\n+\n+### RC-skipped — BashTool subprocess hang (hypothesis 1)\n+\n+Not the root cause. `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.3.14/tool/bash.go:15`\n+installs a 120s default wall-clock timeout (`context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)`)\n+and calls `exec.CommandContext`. Per-call bash hangs self-terminate at 2min.\n+\n+### RC-skipped — Tracker-side claim leak (hypothesis 3)\n+\n+Tracker state is incidental to the hang: the bead's own description notes the\n+hung workers exist as processes (`ps aux | grep claude`). The worker process is\n+alive; the claim is alive because the worker is alive. Fixing the claim alone\n+does not kill the worker. This hypothesis is downstream, not upstream.\n+\n+### RC-skipped — Worktree file-lock (hypothesis 4)\n+\n+No evidence in bundles. Execute-bead worktrees are plain git worktrees at\n+`/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/*`; no process-held file locks would survive across 33–142h\n+without the owning process. If the process is alive, the lock is alive — same\n+as RC3.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 3. Relationship to ddx-b808df39\n+\n+ddx-b808df39 proposes an operator `ddx agent workers stop <id>` command that\n+sends **SIGTERM at the OS level**, then SIGKILL after a grace period. That\n+design **does** work around RC1 (process-level signal bypasses the broken ctx\n+plumbing). So ddx-b808df39 is a *correct mitigation* — but only a mitigation.\n+\n+Without fixing RC1 (and at least RC2), operators will keep needing to invoke\n+`workers stop` manually. The autonomous remediations every other system relies\n+on (cancel ctx, idle timeout, circuit breaker) are each broken today in ways\n+that require explicit code changes, not just a new operator tool.\n+\n+**Recommendation:** Ship ddx-b808df39 first (quick operational relief), then\n+resolve the child beads filed below in order RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 4. Follow-up beads filed\n+\n+Each addresses one root cause with a deterministic acceptance test. Priority\n+and ID are shown; full details available via `ddx bead show <id>`.\n+\n+The IDs below carry an `.execute-bead-wt-…` prefix because `ddx bead create`\n+was invoked from inside the isolated execute-bead worktree — this is a\n+separate DDx bug worth noting but orthogonal to the hang investigation. They\n+are parented to `ddx-0a651925` and resolvable by `ddx bead list --parent ddx-0a651925`.\n+\n+| RC  | Priority | Bead ID (suffix)                                                           | Title                                                              |\n+| --- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n+| RC1 | P0       | `…-526efaf1` | propagate caller context through Runner.Run and RunAgent           |\n+| RC2 | P0       | `…-b203c889` | add wall-clock deadline alongside idle timeout for agent harness   |\n+| RC3 | P2       | `…-b0562380` | bound compaction-stuck breaker by wall-clock time                  |\n+| RC4 | P1       | `…-3e3913a0` | audit and enforce per-request HTTP deadlines on agent provider calls |\n+| —   | P1       | `…-34d039b7` | autonomous worker watchdog with process-level reaper (complements ddx-b808df39) |\n+\n+**Suggested execution order:** RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3 → watchdog. RC1 is a\n+prerequisite for RC4 being meaningful.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 5. What this investigation could NOT do\n+\n+- Capture live `ps`/`pprof` from a hung worker — none was accessible from the\n+  isolated execute-bead worktree. RC4 in particular needs a live `go tool pprof`\n+  goroutine dump to confirm that the stuck goroutine is in `net/http` streaming\n+  read, not some other path.\n+- Confirm whether the agent library's providers install any timeout that was\n+  missed in grep. The listed RC4 bead should start with a provider-path audit\n+  rather than an immediate fix.","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"7c4457b5-7817-4bd4-9850-94282c2cfdb7","uuid":"b9b08566-e074-49f2-a31f-e547a318b057","timestamp":"2026-04-18T04:51:23.303Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"commit 13cd213e157cd211c48f51796bd62a2fb8b1fcae\nAuthor: Test User <test@ddx.test>\nDate:   Sat Apr 18 00:49:37 2026 -0400\n\n    docs(agent): investigate root cause of 33–142h hung workers [ddx-0a651925]\n    \n    Root-cause investigation report for hung execute-bead workers observed on\n    agent projects (33h–142h). No live hung worker was available\n+in this isolated worktree to capture live `ps`/`pprof`, so the incident evidence is\n+drawn from `.ddx/executions/*` bundles and the worker/runner source path.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 1. Observed evidence (from `.ddx/executions/`)\n+\n+| Metric                                               | Count / value                                        |\n+| ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |\n+| Total attempt bundles                                | 328                                                  |\n+| Bundles with `result.json`                           | 291 (89%)                                            |\n+| Bundles with `manifest.json` but **no** `result.json` | 37 (11%) — executions whose worker never finalized  |\n+\n+Top five longest *finalized* attempts:\n+\n+| `duration_ms` | Wall time    | `detail`                                                                    | Bundle                          |\n+| ------------- | ------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |\n+| 7,720,908     | **2h 8m**    | `agent: compaction stuck: 50 consecutive failed compaction attempts`        | 20260416T230316-efbf91c5        |\n+| 7,200,098     | **2h 0m**    | `timeout after 2h0m0s` (claude streaming wall-clock fired)                  | 20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba        |\n+| 6,764,927     | **1h 53m**   | success (agent harness, qwen)                                               | 20260416T230315-f9f46822        |\n+| 5,649,987     | **1h 34m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260416T230313-e193942b        |\n+| 4,284,347     | **1h 11m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260415T103422-bb3a45fa        |\n+\n+**Key observation:** the 33h / 142h hang durations cited in ddx-b808df39 are\n+~15× and ~65× longer than the longest *finalized* attempt on record. This means\n+the hung workers never reached the point of writing `result.json` — they were\n+blocked inside `runner.Run` (or deeper) for days. Every code path that\n+*should* have terminated them (idle timeout, compaction-stuck, stall detector,\n+cancel propagation) failed to fire.\n+\n+The 37 manifest-only bundles are candidates for hung or externally-killed\n+workers; e.g. `20260416T215953-6b515c05` has a manifest from 2026-04-16T21:59Z\n+with no result. Those fit the profile of \"workers running 33h–142h with no bead\n+assigned\" reported on agent projects.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 2. Root-cause classification (source code evidence)\n+\n+The investigation checked the four hypotheses from the bead description.\n+\n+### RC1 (PRIMARY) — Caller context is discarded inside the Runner\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (`Runner.Run`):\n+```go\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+```\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (`Runner.RunAgent`):\n+```go\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+```\n+\n+`RunOptions` (types.go) takes no `context.Context` at all. `agent.ExecuteBead`\n+likewise does not accept ctx, and `server.WorkerManager.runWorker` passes its\n+own ctx into `worker.Run(ctx, …)` but that ctx **never reaches the agent\n+invocation**.\n+\n+**Impact:** `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (workers.go:648) calls `handle.cancel()`\n+which cancels only the worker goroutine's ctx. The running agent provider call\n+/ subprocess is detached. The only way to interrupt a stuck agent today is a\n+SIGKILL from outside the process. This is the structural defect that enables\n+33–142h hangs — once the agent is stuck, nothing inside DDx can stop it.\n+\n+### RC2 — Idle-reset timer, not wall-clock deadline\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/executor.go:35-40` creates an *idle timeout* context value:\n+```go\n+func withExecutionTimeout(ctx context.Context, timeout time.Duration) context.Context {\n+    return context.WithValue(ctx, executionTimeoutKey{}, timeout)\n+}\n+```\n+\n+`executor.go:154-178` consumes it and **resets the timer on every stdout/stderr\n+byte**. `agent_runner.go:264-285` implements the same pattern against agentlib\n+events.\n+\n+The default timeout is 2h (`types.go:363 DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000`). The\n+comment claims \"2 hours — long enough for any task; supervisor/agent decides\n+termination\" but the actual behaviour is **\"2 hours of silence required to fire\"**.\n+A provider that emits a heartbeat, a ping, a retry line, or any byte resets the\n+timer indefinitely.\n+\n+The `runClaudeStreaming` path (`claude_stream.go:373-384`) is the only path that\n+uses a wall-clock deadline — and it is the only path we see actually hitting\n+the timeout (bundle 20260415T015231 exactly 2h 0m 0.098s).\n+\n+**Impact:** for `harness=agent` (qwen, minimax, openrouter, etc.), no wall-clock\n+bound exists. 33–142h hangs are consistent with a provider that dribbles events\n+slowly enough to defeat the idle reset.\n+\n+### RC3 — Compaction-stuck breaker fires late\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:218-241` increments a counter on each no-op\n+compaction event and trips at `stuckThreshold = maxConsecutiveCompactionFailures`\n+(50 by default). On observed `efbf91c5` it took **2h 8m** to trip. If one\n+iteration takes 3 minutes to produce a no-op compaction event, 50 events =\n+~150 min — matches the observed tail.\n+\n+**Impact:** on its own, the breaker is too slow to be a useful hang-guard. It\n+should be replaced or complemented by a wall-clock-bounded variant.\n+\n+### RC4 — Provider HTTP requests likely lack a per-request deadline\n+\n+`RunAgent` passes the internal `context.Background()`-derived ctx into\n+`agentlib.Run(ctx, req)`. The agent library's providers (`openai.Client`,\n+anthropic client) do not appear to install a per-request deadline themselves\n+(grepping the v0.3.14 module cache shows short timeouts on discovery/probe\n+only; the main call path has no WithTimeout). A stalled TCP socket after\n+headers are received but before the response body arrives can hang a streaming\n+read indefinitely.\n+\n+**Impact:** consistent with \"alive but mid-LLM-call with no client-side\n+deadline\" — hypothesis (2) in the bead description. Cannot be fully confirmed\n+from source alone; needs runtime `go tool pprof http://localhost:<port>/debug/pprof/goroutine`\n+against a live hung worker.\n+\n+### RC-skipped — BashTool subprocess hang (hypothesis 1)\n+\n+Not the root cause. `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.3.14/tool/bash.go:15`\n+installs a 120s default wall-clock timeout (`context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)`)\n+and calls `exec.CommandContext`. Per-call bash hangs self-terminate at 2min.\n+\n+### RC-skipped — Tracker-side claim leak (hypothesis 3)\n+\n+Tracker state is incidental to the hang: the bead's own description notes the\n+hung workers exist as processes (`ps aux | grep claude`). The worker process is\n+alive; the claim is alive because the worker is alive. Fixing the claim alone\n+does not kill the worker. This hypothesis is downstream, not upstream.\n+\n+### RC-skipped — Worktree file-lock (hypothesis 4)\n+\n+No evidence in bundles. Execute-bead worktrees are plain git worktrees at\n+`/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/*`; no process-held file locks would survive across 33–142h\n+without the owning process. If the process is alive, the lock is alive — same\n+as RC3.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 3. Relationship to ddx-b808df39\n+\n+ddx-b808df39 proposes an operator `ddx agent workers stop <id>` command that\n+sends **SIGTERM at the OS level**, then SIGKILL after a grace period. That\n+design **does** work around RC1 (process-level signal bypasses the broken ctx\n+plumbing). So ddx-b808df39 is a *correct mitigation* — but only a mitigation.\n+\n+Without fixing RC1 (and at least RC2), operators will keep needing to invoke\n+`workers stop` manually. The autonomous remediations every other system relies\n+on (cancel ctx, idle timeout, circuit breaker) are each broken today in ways\n+that require explicit code changes, not just a new operator tool.\n+\n+**Recommendation:** Ship ddx-b808df39 first (quick operational relief), then\n+resolve the child beads filed below in order RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 4. Follow-up beads filed\n+\n+Each addresses one root cause with a deterministic acceptance test. Priority\n+and ID are shown; full details available via `ddx bead show <id>`.\n+\n+The IDs below carry an `.execute-bead-wt-…` prefix because `ddx bead create`\n+was invoked from inside the isolated execute-bead worktree — this is a\n+separate DDx bug worth noting but orthogonal to the hang investigation. They\n+are parented to `ddx-0a651925` and resolvable by `ddx bead list --parent ddx-0a651925`.\n+\n+| RC  | Priority | Bead ID (suffix)                                                           | Title                                                              |\n+| --- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n+| RC1 | P0       | `…-526efaf1` | propagate caller context through Runner.Run and RunAgent           |\n+| RC2 | P0       | `…-b203c889` | add wall-clock deadline alongside idle timeout for agent harness   |\n+| RC3 | P2       | `…-b0562380` | bound compaction-stuck breaker by wall-clock time                  |\n+| RC4 | P1       | `…-3e3913a0` | audit and enforce per-request HTTP deadlines on agent provider calls |\n+| —   | P1       | `…-34d039b7` | autonomous worker watchdog with process-level reaper (complements ddx-b808df39) |\n+\n+**Suggested execution order:** RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3 → watchdog. RC1 is a\n+prerequisite for RC4 being meaningful.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 5. What this investigation could NOT do\n+\n+- Capture live `ps`/`pprof` from a hung worker — none was accessible from the\n+  isolated execute-bead worktree. RC4 in particular needs a live `go tool pprof`\n+  goroutine dump to confirm that the stuck goroutine is in `net/http` streaming\n+  read, not some other path.\n+- Confirm whether the agent library's providers install any timeout that was\n+  missed in grep. The listed RC4 bead should start with a provider-path audit\n+  rather than an immediate fix.","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

Report at .ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md.

Root-cause summary:
- RC1 (PRIMARY): Runner.Run and Runner.RunAgent discard caller ctx (runner.go:239, agent_runner.go:163). WorkerManager.Stop cannot interrupt a running agent.
- RC2: idle-reset timer instead of wall-clock deadline for non-claude paths (executor.go:154, agent_runner.go:264).
- RC3: compaction-stuck breaker fires too late (~2h observed).
- RC4: provider HTTP calls likely lack per-request deadlines (confirm against live hung worker).
- BashTool subprocess hang ruled out (120s default timeout). Tracker-side / fs-lock hypotheses are downstream of RC1.

Follow-up beads filed (parent=ddx-0a651925):
- …-526efaf1 P0 [RC1] propagate caller context
- …-b203c889 P0 [RC2] wall-clock deadline
- …-3e3913a0 P1 [RC4] per-request HTTP deadlines
- …-b0562380 P2 [RC3] bound compaction-stuck breaker by wall-clock
- …-34d039b7 P1 autonomous worker watchdog (complements ddx-b808df39)

ddx-b808df39's operator stop/kill is a correct mitigation but NOT sufficient as sole fix — until RC1 lands, operators will keep needing it. Ship ddx-b808df39 first, then RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3 → watchdog.

95:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go exports withHeartbeat(ctx, beadID, interval, store, fn) with the signature defined above.
2. execute_bead_loop.go:732-753 replaced with a single withHeartbeat call; no inline goroutine + WaitGroup remains in the loop body.
3. Drain loop caller of withHeartbeat is the production site (execute_bead_loop.go or its successor after C13 rename).
4. TestWithHeartbeat_TickerFiredBeforeReturn in cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat_test.go: injects a stub ticker; verifies Heartbeat called at least once before fn returns.
5. TestWithHeartbeat_CancelPropagates: cancels ctx before fn returns; verifies goroutine exits without blocking.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/agent/work/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28.569636417Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0b8780db","depends_on_id":"ddx-06eb05d8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
  The heartbeat goroutine in the drain loop is inlined, coupling ticker lifecycle management with candidate execution and blocking unit tests from verifying heartbeat behavior without a real ticker.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-753 inlines a goroutine + sync.WaitGroup + time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval) directly in the loop body. hbCtx and hbWG are declared locally; the goroutine calls w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID) on every tick. There is no injection seam, so tests cannot stub the ticker to verify heartbeat cadence or cancellation without real wall-clock delays.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extract to withHeartbeat(ctx context.Context, beadID string, interval time.Duration, store interface{ Heartbeat(string) error }, fn func() (ExecuteBeadReport, error)) error in a new file cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go.
  - withHeartbeat starts the internal goroutine + WaitGroup, calls fn(), cancels the ticker goroutine on fn return, and returns fn's result.
  - Drain loop body (execute_bead_loop.go:732) replaces the inline goroutine block with a single withHeartbeat call.
  - Add TestWithHeartbeat_TickerFiredBeforeReturn and TestWithHeartbeat_CancelPropagates to cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat_test.go using a stub ticker channel injection.

NON-SCOPE
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Changes to heartbeatInterval policy or the Store.Heartbeat call itself.
  - Any behavior change to the drain loop; this is a pure extraction.","id":"ddx-0b8780db","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","refactor","kind:refactor"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"C11: heartbeat extracted to withHeartbeat helper","updated_at":"2026-05-03T20:09:40.552661339Z"}
123:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer browsing the DDx web UI, I want the documents list to show only canonical project documents under the configured roots, so I can find `docs/resources/agent-harness-ac.md` without seeing duplicates from agent worktrees or broken absolute-path links.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. `findMarkdownFiles` skips `.claude` (in addition to `.git` and `.ddx`) and does not descend into any `worktrees/` directory regardless of parent.
2. `doc.Path` stored in the graph is relative to `workingDir`; no document path begins with `/`.
3. GraphQL `documents` query returns zero entries whose path contains `.claude/worktrees/` when the repo has active worktrees under `.claude/worktrees/`.
4. GraphQL `document(id:)` resolver handles both the previously-absolute form and the new relative form for a grace period (read-side tolerance) OR all callers are updated atomically.
5. Web UI document route `/documents/:path` renders the correct document content for `docs/resources/agent-harness-ac.md` (no double slash, no 404).
6. Unit test in `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go` creates a fixture with `.claude/worktrees/agent-x/docs/foo.md` and `docs/foo.md`, and asserts only the latter appears in the graph with a relative path.
7. Playwright e2e test in `cli/internal/server/frontend/` navigates to the documents list, asserts no listed document has a path containing `.claude/` or starting with `/`, clicks a real `docs/` document, and verifies the content renders.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"4f3ff4e9592238fee90f9682e57dfe1fb99b1325","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:06:21.727193482Z","description":"## Observed

Browsing the web UI to a document URL renders `Document not found` instead of content. The URL contains a worktree path with a leading double slash, e.g.:

  /nodes/<node>/projects/<proj>/documents//Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.claude/worktrees/agent-a0673989/docs/resources/agent-harness-ac.md

Two distinct defects:

1. **`.claude/worktrees/` pollution** — the documents list surfaces markdown files from AI agent worktrees checked out under `.claude/worktrees/`. These are throwaway copies of the repo; they are noise and frequently shadow / duplicate the real `docs/` tree.
2. **Absolute document paths** — document IDs/paths are absolute (`/Users/erik/...`), producing malformed URLs (leading double slash) and failing the reader resolver.

## Root cause

`cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:247` (`findMarkdownFiles`) walks `workingDir` and only skips `.git` and `.ddx`. It traverses `.claude/` (including `.claude/worktrees/<agent>/…`) and records every `.md` it finds.

`cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:128` stores `doc.Path = filepath.Clean(filePath)`. Because `filepath.Walk` hands back paths rooted at the absolute `workingDir`, `doc.Path` ends up absolute. The GraphQL document resolver surfaces this absolute path as the document's id/path, which the frontend then interpolates into a URL.

## Scope of fix

- Exclude `.claude/` (and any `worktrees/` subtree) from doc discovery. `.claude` is a tool-managed directory analogous to `.git`/`.ddx`; it is not a source of canonical project documents.
- Normalize `doc.Path` to be relative to `workingDir` so routes stay clean and portable.
- No change required on the frontend beyond existing URL handling once paths are relative.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"omlx-vidar-1235","resolved_model":"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b","fallback_chain":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:00:02.172732523Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=omlx-vidar-1235 model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b probe=ok

rationale: Bead ddx-27936512 is a duplicate of work already merged in commit 0b1b3964
("feat(server): run-detail resolver + tool-call persistence + bundle-file
confinement [ddx-6d132f62]"). All four acceptance criteria are already
satisfied by existing code:

AC1: Run resolver returns full detail (lossless)
  - cli/internal/server/state_runs_detail.go:250 loadRunDetail() populates
    prompt/response/stderr from session bodies and bundleFiles via
    listRunBundleFiles(); invoked from state_runs.go:520 on run(id:) lookup.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs.go wires Run, RunToolCalls,
    RunBundleFile resolvers to the state methods.

AC2: ToolCallEntry persisted at drain time (NOT log-parsing)
  - agent.SessionIndexEntry carries a ToolCalls []ToolCallEntry field;
    SessionIndexEntryFromResult copies result.ToolCalls into the entry,
    so the persistence happens once at drain time rather than on each
    resolver call. GetRunToolCallsGraphQL reads only the persisted slice.

AC3: bundleFile endpoint refuses path traversal
  - resolveRunBundlePath (state_runs_detail.go:111) rejects absolute paths,
    "..", paths whose Clean escapes the root, and symlink targets that
    EvalSymlinks resolves outside bundleRoot. Surfaced via
    GetRunBundleFileGraphQL → Query.runBundleFile.

AC4: Tests cover all three
  - state_runs_detail_test.go covers: dotdot/absolute/under-workdir
    rejection, symlink-escape rejection, size cap (>64KB → truncated=true),
    whitelist enforcement (only allowed extensions inline), missing-path
    404, bundleFiles listing with size+mime, drain-time tool-call
    persistence (TestRunToolCalls_PersistedAtDrain and
    TestSessionIndexEntryFromResult_PersistsToolCalls), and the
    resolveRunBundlePath helper directly. All pass at base-rev b10e224f.

verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/server/ -run 'TestRunBundleFile|TestRunToolCalls|TestSessionIndexEntryFromResult|TestResolveRunBundlePath|TestRunBundleFiles_Listing' -count=1

2. execute_bead_loop.go:847 block replaced with try.Attempt delegation; no direct RunConflictRecovery call remains in the loop.
3. TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome in cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go passes: stub executor returns a conflict report, Attempt returns Outcome with disposition Merged.
4. TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark in cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go passes: auto-recover returns error, Attempt returns OutcomePark.

  The conflict-recovery state machine (auto-merge + focused-resolve + close-or-park) is partially extracted but the description cites only the pre-extraction loop lines, leaving the executor without a clear picture of the current API boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:118 defines RunConflictRecovery(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput) ConflictRecoveryOutput — the C4 extraction already landed. The bead description still refers to the old inline branch. The caller site is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 (loop branches on ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery, then calls RunConflictRecovery). The try.Attempt API (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:67, func Attempt(ctx, store, beadID, AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error)) does not yet call RunConflictRecovery; conflict recovery remains loop-owned.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Migrate RunConflictRecovery invocation from execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 into try.Attempt (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go) so Attempt's return Outcome already encodes conflict-recovery disposition.
  - Add try/conflict_recovery.go: thin adapter from ConflictRecoveryOutput to Outcome (Disposition=Merged → OutcomeSuccess, Park/NeedsHuman → OutcomePark).
  - Loop caller (execute_bead_loop.go:847) delegates to try.Attempt instead of calling RunConflictRecovery directly; removes the inline branch.
  - Add TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome and TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark to cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Deleting the attempted/hookFailed maps (C7, ddx-06eb05d8).
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).

  The execute-bead subsystem files use execute_bead_* naming that conflates the orchestrator, the attempt, the review, and the loop under one prefix, making package boundaries and import graphs ambiguous. The refactor proposal was stored at /tmp/ and is unreadable post-session.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (drain loop), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (attempt), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (land coordinator), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (landing), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (review), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go (review triage), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go (disposition), and cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go (mirror) all share a flat package with unrelated concerns. The ddx try CLI command named in FEAT-010 does not exist (cli/cmd/try.go absent).

PROPOSED FIX
  Rename per the following table (no behavior change):
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go             -> cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go (already exists; merge or verify)
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/land.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/coordinator.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/review.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/review_triage.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/disposition.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go         -> cli/internal/agent/work/drain.go
    cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/mirror.go

  Rename identifiers: ExecuteBeadWorker -> work.Worker, ExecuteBeadWorker.Run -> work.Worker.Drain, ExecuteBead -> try.Attempt (FEAT-006 + FEAT-010).

  Add cli/cmd/try.go with cobra command "ddx try <bead-id>" invoking try.Attempt directly (FEAT-010 surface).

  Update FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 spec docs to reflect new package paths. Keep ExecuteBead* shims as typedefs; deletion is C14.

NON-SCOPE
  - Behavior changes to the drain loop or attempt logic.
  - Deletion of ExecuteBead* typedefs (C14).
